162	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
163	
164	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
165	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
166	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction. The
167	construction path (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): restrict to a chart through the
168	open-immersion pullback equivalence (`Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso`) and glue
169	the local sections by locality of sections (`existsUnique_gluing'`).
170	
171	NOTE (scaffold): the body and the module-cocycle hypotheses on `g` are still to be filled;
172	the transition data `g` (per-overlap pullback isos) is recorded in the signature, the
173	multiplicative cocycle conditions remain to be added before the construction is closed. -/
174	
175	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
176	
177	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
178	
179	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
180	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
181	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
182	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
183	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
184	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
185	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
186	
187	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
188	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
189	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
190	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
191	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
192	
193	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
194	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
195	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
196	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
197	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
198	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
199	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
200	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
201	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
202	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
203	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
204	
205	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
206	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
207	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
208	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
209	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
210	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
211	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
212	
213	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
214	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
215	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
216	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
217	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
218	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
219	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
220	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
221	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
222	    Category.assoc]
223	
224	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
225	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
226	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
227	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
228	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
229	the module cocycle (C2). -/
230	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
231	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
232	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
233	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
234	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
235	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
236	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
237	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
238	
239	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
240	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
241	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
242	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
243	
244	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
245	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
246	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
247	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
248	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
249	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
250	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
251	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
252	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
253	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
254	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
255	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
256	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
257	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
258	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
259	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
260	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
261	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
262	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
263	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
264	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
265	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
266	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
267	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
268	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
269	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
270	    D.glued.Modules :=
271	  sorry
272	
273	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
274	
275	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
276	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
277	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
278	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
279	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
280	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
281	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
282	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
283	morphisms. -/
284	
285	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
286	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
287	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
288	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
289	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
290	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
291	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
292	  constructor
293	  intro V
294	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
295	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
296	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
297	  apply zigzag_isConnected
298	  intro s t
299	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
300	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
301	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
302	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
303	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
304	
305	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
306	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
307	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
308	Project-local. -/
309	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
310	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
311	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
312	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
313	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
314	
315	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
316	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
317	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
318	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
319	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
320	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
321	Project-local. -/
322	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
323	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
324	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
325	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
326	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
327	  intro i
328	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
329	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
330	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
331	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
332	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
333	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
334	
335	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
336	
337	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
338	
339	/-! ## The universal quotient sheaf and the tautological quotient -/
340	
341	/-- The **universal quotient sheaf** `U` on `Gr(d,r)` (`def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf`):
342	the rank-`d` locally free sheaf obtained by gluing the free rank-`d` chart sheaves
343	`O_{U^I}^d` along the bundle transition cocycle `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`.
344	
345	NOTE (scaffold): rides on `Scheme.Modules.glue`; body to be filled once `glue` lands. -/
346	noncomputable def universalQuotient (d r : ℕ) : (scheme d r).Modules :=
347	  sorry
348	
349	/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
350	surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
351	the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`.
352	
353	NOTE (scaffold): rides on `Scheme.Modules.glue`; body to be filled once `glue` lands. -/
354	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
355	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
356	  sorry
357	
358	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
359	
360	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
361	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
362	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
363	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
364	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
365	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
366	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
367	
368	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
369	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
370	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
371	unchanged. -/
372	
373	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
374	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
375	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
376	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
377	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
378	  F : T.Modules
379	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
380	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
381	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
382	  epi : Epi q
383	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
384	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
385	
386	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
387	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
388	coincide). -/
389	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
390	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
391	
392	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
393	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
394	
395	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
396	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
397	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
398	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
399	
400	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
401	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
402	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
403	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
404	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
405	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
406	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
407	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
408	
409	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
410	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
411	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
412	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩
413	
414	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
415	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
416	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
417	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
418	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
419	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
420	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
421	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
422	  epi :=
423	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
424	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
425	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
426	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
427	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q)
428	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ) inferInstance _ _
429	        x.q x.epi)
430	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree
431	
432	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/
433	lemma rqPullback_rel {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
434	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
435	    (rqPullback ψ x).Rel (rqPullback ψ y) := by
436	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
437	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).mapIso f, ?_⟩
438	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) ≫
439	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map f.hom
440	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map y.q
441	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map_comp]
442	  exact congrArg
443	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map m) hf
444	
445	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
446	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
447	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
448	
449	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
450	`map_comp`) are reduced — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
451	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — to the free-sheaf coherences
452	`pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) = (pullbackId T).app (free _)` and the analogue for a composite,
453	which in turn reduce to the unit-level identity `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) =
454	(pullbackId).app unit` (and its composite analogue). These remaining coherences between
455	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` are
456	`whnf`-heavy and left as the sole open obstacle. -/
457	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
458	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
459	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
460	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
461	  map_id X := by
462	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
463	    ext z
464	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
465	    | _ x =>
466	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
467	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
468	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
469	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
470	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
471	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
472	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
473	      sorry
474	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
475	    ext z
476	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
477	    | _ x =>
478	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
479	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
480	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
481	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
482	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
483	      -- through `pullbackComp` — the composite analogue of the `map_id` obstacle.
484	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
485	      sorry
486	
487	/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
488	the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
489	quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.
490	
491	NOTE (scaffold): body (the local-to-global inverse construction of Nitsure §1) to be
492	filled once `functor`, `tautologicalQuotient`, and `Scheme.Modules.glue` land. -/
493	noncomputable def represents (d r : ℕ) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
494	    (functor d r).RepresentableBy (scheme d r) :=
495	  sorry
496	
497	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
498

1	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Basic
2	import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Map
3	import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.Associator
4	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Equalizers
5	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal
6	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Localization
8	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf
10	
11	/-!
12	# Section graded ring infrastructure, Layer 1: tensor powers of a sheaf of modules
13	
14	This file builds the Mathlib-absent infrastructure of
15	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`, Layer 1
16	(`sec:sgr_tensor_powers`): the tensor product, tensor powers, and twists of
17	sheaves of modules over a scheme `X`, together with the unitor and braiding
18	isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
19	
20	The category `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` of sheaves of modules
21	over a scheme carries **no** monoidal structure in Mathlib (the structure sheaf
22	varies the base ring over opens).  Mathlib *does* supply:
23	
24	* the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of **presheaves** of modules
25	  `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
26	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal`), and
27	* the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`
28	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification`).
29	
30	We therefore build the tensor product of sheaves of modules as the sheafification
31	of the objectwise (presheaf) tensor product, following
32	[Stacks, Tag 01CA].
33	
34	## Main definitions
35	
36	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification` — the scheme-level
37	  sheafification functor `X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules`.
38	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) —
39	  `F ⊗ G := (F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)^#`.
40	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow` (`def:sheafTensorPow`) —
41	  the `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`) —
43	  the `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}`.
44	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCounitIso` — the reflective
45	  counit iso `(F.toPresheaf)^# ≅ F`.
46	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjUnitIso`,
47	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjRightUnitor`,
48	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorBraiding` — the left/right unitor and
49	  braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
50	
51	The comparison isomorphism `L^{⊗m} ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗(m+m')}`
52	(`lem:sheafTensorPow_add`) is **deferred**: see the handoff note before the end
53	of the file for the single missing ingredient (strong-monoidality of the module
54	sheafification functor) and the launching pad assembled here.
55	-/
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory Limits
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
62	
63	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
64	
65	/-- The scheme-level sheafification functor, sending a presheaf of modules over a
66	scheme `X` to its associated sheaf of modules `X.Modules`.  It is the
67	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification` functor for the identity morphism of the
68	underlying presheaf of rings (which is locally bijective).  Non-private because it
69	appears in the statement of `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
70	noncomputable def sheafification : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules :=
71	  PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
72	
73	/-- The category `X.PresheafOfModules` of presheaves of modules over a scheme,
74	presented in the exact form `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`
75	for which Mathlib equips it with a symmetric monoidal structure.  This is
76	*definitionally* `X.PresheafOfModules` (since
77	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`), so a term of
78	either type is accepted for the other. -/
79	private abbrev MonoidalPresheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ :=
80	  _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
81	
82	/-- The tensor product of two sheaves of modules over a scheme, defined as the
83	sheafification of the objectwise tensor product presheaf
84	(Mathlib `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  See [Stacks, Tag 01CA]
85	(`def:sheafTensorObj`). -/
86	noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
87	  sheafification.obj
88	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
89	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
90	
91	/-- The structure sheaf as a sheaf of modules over itself: the unit object of the
92	tensor product, i.e. the zeroth tensor power `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X`
93	(`lem:moduleUnit_mathlib`). -/
94	private noncomputable abbrev unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) : X.Modules :=
95	  SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf
96	
97	/-- The `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules over a scheme, defined
98	by recursion: `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X` (the unit module) and
99	`L^{⊗(m+1)} = L^{⊗m} ⊗ L`.  See [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`def:sheafTensorPow`). -/
100	noncomputable def tensorPow (L : X.Modules) : ℕ → X.Modules
101	  | 0 => unitModule X
102	  | (m + 1) => tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L
103	
104	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_zero (L : X.Modules) : tensorPow L 0 = unitModule X := rfl
105	
106	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_succ (L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) :
107	    tensorPow L (m + 1) = tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L := rfl
108	
109	/-- The `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules `F` by the `m`-th
110	tensor power of a line bundle `L` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`).  This is the
111	degree-`m` carrier of the section graded module. -/
112	noncomputable def moduleTensorPow (F L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) : X.Modules :=
113	  tensorObj F (tensorPow L m)
114	
115	@[simp] private lemma moduleTensorPow_zero (F L : X.Modules) :
116	    moduleTensorPow F L 0 = tensorObj F (unitModule X) := rfl
117	
118	/-! ### Unitor and braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product
119	
120	These are the parts of the (would-be) symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
121	that descend through sheafification from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
122	using only *functoriality* of `sheafification` (and, for the unitors, the
123	reflective counit iso) — no strong-monoidality of `sheafification` is needed, so
124	they are axiom-clean.  They are the launching pad for `tensorPowAdd`. -/
125	
126	/-- The counit isomorphism of the module sheafification adjunction: sheafifying
127	the underlying presheaf of a sheaf of modules returns the sheaf itself.  This is
128	an isomorphism because the counit of `sheafification ⊣ toPresheafOfModules` is
129	invertible (the right adjoint `SheafOfModules.forget` is fully faithful).  It is
130	the launching pad for the left-unitor base case of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
131	private noncomputable def sheafificationCounitIso (G : X.Modules) :
132	    sheafification.obj ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ≅ G :=
133	  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
134	    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).counit).app G
135	
136	/-- The left-unitor isomorphism `unitModule X ⊗ G ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
137	product: the presheaf left unitor `λ_` descended through sheafification, composed
138	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  This is the base case (`m = 0`) of
139	`tensorPowAdd`.  Axiom-clean. -/
140	private noncomputable def tensorObjUnitIso (G : X.Modules) :
141	    tensorObj (unitModule X) G ≅ G :=
142	  sheafification.mapIso
143	      (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
144	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
145	    sheafificationCounitIso G
146	
147	/-- The right-unitor isomorphism `G ⊗ unitModule X ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
148	product: the presheaf right unitor `ρ_` descended through sheafification, composed
149	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  Axiom-clean (no monoidal structure
150	on `X.Modules` is required). -/
151	private noncomputable def tensorObjRightUnitor (G : X.Modules) :
152	    tensorObj G (unitModule X) ≅ G :=
153	  sheafification.mapIso
154	      (MonoidalCategory.rightUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
155	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
156	    sheafificationCounitIso G
157	
158	/-- The braiding isomorphism `F ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ F` of the sheaf tensor product,
159	descended through sheafification from the symmetric braiding on
160	`X.PresheafOfModules` (`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  Axiom-clean: the
161	braiding is pure sheafification-functoriality of the presheaf-level braiding, so
162	no monoidal structure on `X.Modules` is required.  This is the symmetry used in
163	the inductive step of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
164	private noncomputable def tensorBraiding (F G : X.Modules) :
165	    tensorObj F G ≅ tensorObj G F :=
166	  sheafification.mapIso
167	    (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
168	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
169	
170	/-! ### Lax-monoidal global sections: the section multiplication
171	
172	The global-sections functor `Γ(X, -)` is only *lax* monoidal: a pair of global
173	sections does not commute with sheafification, so the multiplication is a map,
174	not an isomorphism.  It is nonetheless `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)`-linear and is the data the
175	section graded ring is built from. -/
176	
177	/-- The **section multiplication** (`def:sectionMul`), the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear map
178	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G) → Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.
179	
180	Its domain `(F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)(X)` is, by the objectwise formula of
181	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-module
182	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G)` of elementary tensors of global sections; a pair
183	`(σ, τ)` is sent to `σ ⊗ τ`.  Postcomposing with the global-sections component of
184	the sheafification unit `η : P → P^#` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) lands in
185	`Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.  As a morphism in `ModuleCat (Γ(X,𝒪_X))` it is automatically
186	`Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear; this records that linearity.  Axiom-clean: it is pure
187	sheafification-unit naturality, requiring no monoidal structure on `X.Modules`. -/
188	noncomputable def sectionsMul (F G : X.Modules) :
189	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
190	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).obj (Opposite.op ⊤) ⟶
191	      (tensorObj F G).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
192	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
193	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
194	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))).app (Opposite.op ⊤)
195	
196	/-! ### The strong-monoidality comparison `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`
197	
198	Following `analogies/snap-route.md` (Analogue 1) and the blueprint proof of
199	`lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: module sheafification is the
200	localization functor at the class `W := J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)` of
201	morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying abelian-presheaf morphism is a
202	local isomorphism (a `J.W` for the opens topology `J` on `X`). -/
203	
204	/-- The Grothendieck topology on the opens of the scheme `X`. -/
205	private abbrev opensTopology (X : Scheme.{u}) : GrothendieckTopology (TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :=
206	  Opens.grothendieckTopology (X : TopCat)
207	
208	open MorphismProperty in
209	/-- **Localization criterion for module sheafification.**  The scheme-level module
210	sheafification `sheafification.map f` is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules iff the
211	underlying abelian-presheaf morphism `(PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map f` lies in
212	the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology (i.e. is a local isomorphism of
213	abelian-group presheaves).  This is the reduction step of
214	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: it turns the strong-monoidality comparison
215	into a purely abelian local-isomorphism statement.  Project-local: it specialises
216	`_root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` to the
217	identity morphism of `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`. -/
218	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_iff {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ⟶ Q) :
219	    IsIso (sheafification.map f) ↔
220	      (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map f) := by
221	  have e := _root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms
222	      (J := opensTopology X) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
223	  constructor
224	  · intro h
225	    have h' : ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)).inverseImage
226	        (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj))) f := h
227	    rw [← e] at h'
228	    exact h'
229	  · intro h
230	    have h' : (((opensTopology X).W).inverseImage
231	        (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) f := h
232	    rw [e] at h'
233	    exact h'
234	
235	/-- **The sheafification unit is an abelian local isomorphism.**  The underlying
236	abelian-presheaf morphism of the module sheafification unit `η_P : P ⟶ P^#` is
237	*definitionally* the abelian sheafification unit `toSheafify J P.presheaf`
238	(`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app`), which lies
239	in the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology by
240	`GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify`.  Project-local: this is the `η_P ∈ J.W`
241	ingredient of the abelian-`J.W`-monoidality transfer underlying
242	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
243	lemma localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
244	    (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
245	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) := by
246	  rw [PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
247	  exact (opensTopology X).W_toSheafify _
248	
249	/-- **Sheafification inverts the localization unit.**  `sheafification.map η_P` is an
250	isomorphism of sheaves of modules (the reflective-localization unit becomes invertible
251	after sheafifying).  Obtained by feeding `localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit` through the
252	localization criterion `isIso_sheafification_map_iff`.  Project-local: the `m = 0`
253	launching pad and the un-whiskered special case of
254	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
255	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
256	    IsIso (sheafification.map
257	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) :=
258	  (isIso_sheafification_map_iff _).mpr (localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit P)
259	
260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — relative tensor product as a coequalizer
261	
262	This section builds the **objectwise** content of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`
263	(`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`): over a commutative ring `S` and `S`-modules `M, N`,
264	the relative tensor product `M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer, *in the category of abelian
265	groups*, of the two `S`-action maps
266	
267	  `M ⊗[ℤ] S ⊗[ℤ] N  ⇉  M ⊗[ℤ] N`,    `m ⊗ s ⊗ n ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`  /  `m ⊗ (s • n)`.
268	
269	This is the Mathlib-absent brick on which the strong-monoidality comparison
270	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit` rests: the underlying abelian presheaf of the
271	presheaf-level relative tensor `P ⊗_p Q` is, objectwise, exactly this coequalizer.  The
272	universal property is the abelian-group universal property of the relative tensor product,
273	packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`.  Everything here is axiom-clean.
274	
275	The promotion of this objectwise colimit to the presheaf category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrp`
276	(where colimits are computed objectwise) and the identification of the whiskered unit
277	`η_P ▷ Q` with the induced map of coequalizers are the next steps; see the handoff note. -/
278	
279	namespace RelativeTensorCoequalizer
280	
281	open TensorProduct
282	
283	variable (S : Type u) [CommRing S] (M N : Type u)
284	  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
285	
286	/-- The `S`-action map `S ⊗[ℤ] N → N`, `s ⊗ n ↦ s • n`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
287	noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
288	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
289	    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
290	    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
291	
292	/-- The `S`-action map `M ⊗[ℤ] S → M`, `m ⊗ s ↦ s • m`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
293	noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
294	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
295	    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
296	    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
297	
298	/-- Right action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`. -/
299	noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
300	  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
301	
302	/-- Left action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`. -/
303	noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
304	  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp
305	    (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
306	
307	@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
308	    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
309	
310	@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
311	    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
312	
313	/-- The canonical projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N`, `m ⊗ n ↦ m ⊗ n`, as a `ℤ`-linear
314	map.  It is the cofork map exhibiting `M ⊗[S] N` as the coequalizer. -/
315	noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
316	  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
317	    { toFun := fun m =>
318	        (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
319	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
320	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp }
321	    (fun r m n => by simp)).toIntLinearMap
322	
323	@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
324	
325	/-- The projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` is surjective (it is the canonical
326	quotient map onto the relative tensor). -/
327	lemma projL_surjective : Function.Surjective (projL S M N) := by
328	  intro y
329	  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
330	  | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
331	  | tmul m n => exact ⟨m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n, projL_tmul S M N m n⟩
332	  | add a b ha hb =>
333	    obtain ⟨pa, rfl⟩ := ha; obtain ⟨pb, rfl⟩ := hb; exact ⟨pa + pb, map_add _ _ _⟩
334	
335	/-- The two action maps become equal after the projection: this is the cofork
336	coequalizing condition, established at the level of `ℤ`-linear maps. -/
337	lemma projL_comp_act :
338	    (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
339	  apply TensorProduct.ext'; intro m x
340	  induction x with
341	  | zero => rw [tmul_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
342	  | tmul s n =>
343	    change projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
344	      = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
345	    rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul, ← TensorProduct.smul_tmul',
346	      TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
347	  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]
348	
349	/-- Left action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
350	noncomputable def aL :
351	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
352	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
353	/-- Right action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
354	noncomputable def aR :
355	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
356	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
357	/-- The projection as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
358	noncomputable def piMor :
359	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
360	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
361	
362	@[simp] lemma piMor_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (piMor S M N)) x = projL S M N x := rfl
363	
364	instance piMor_epi : Epi (piMor S M N) :=
365	  ConcreteCategory.epi_of_surjective (piMor S M N) (projL_surjective S M N)
366	
367	/-- The projection coequalizes the two action maps (as morphisms of abelian groups). -/
368	lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
369	  ext x; exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
370	
371	/-- The cofork `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` of abelian groups. -/
372	noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
373	  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)
374	
375	/-- The descent map out of `M ⊗[S] N` induced by a cofork `s`: a pair of global
376	sections balanced under the `S`-action factors through the relative tensor.  This
377	is the universal property packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. -/
378	noncomputable def descHom (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
379	    (M ⊗[S] N) →+ s.pt :=
380	  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
381	    { toFun := fun m =>
382	        { toFun := fun n => (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
383	          map_zero' := by rw [tmul_zero, map_zero]
384	          map_add' := fun n1 n2 => by rw [tmul_add, map_add] }
385	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp [zero_tmul]
386	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [add_tmul] }
387	    (fun a m n => by
388	      simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
389	      have key :=
390	        congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))) s.condition
391	      simpa using key)
392	
393	@[simp] lemma descHom_tmul (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) (m : M) (n : N) :
394	    descHom S M N s (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n) := rfl
395	
396	/-- The descent map as a morphism of abelian groups out of the cofork apex. -/
397	noncomputable def descMor (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
398	    (cofork S M N).pt ⟶ s.pt :=
399	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (descHom S M N s)
400	
401	/-- The descent map factors the cofork's projection: `π ≫ descMor s = s.π`. -/
402	lemma descFac (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
403	    (cofork S M N).π ≫ descMor S M N s = s.π := by
404	  ext x
405	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
406	  | zero => simp
407	  | tmul m n =>
408	    change descHom S M N s (projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
409	    rw [projL_tmul, descHom_tmul]
410	  | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
411	
412	/-- **`M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer**, in the category of abelian groups, of the two
413	`S`-action maps `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N`.  This is the objectwise content of
414	`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`; uniqueness uses that the projection `piMor` is an
415	epimorphism.  Axiom-clean. -/
416	noncomputable def isColimitCofork : Limits.IsColimit (cofork S M N) :=
417	  Limits.Cofork.IsColimit.mk _ (descMor S M N) (descFac S M N)
418	    (fun s _ hf => (cancel_epi (piMor S M N)).mp (hf.trans (descFac S M N s).symm))
419	
420	end RelativeTensorCoequalizer

919	        have hut : IsUnit (algebraMap B Bg (t : B)) := IsLocalization.map_units Bg t
920	        have ht_eq : algebraMap B Bg (↑t) = ↑(hu.unit ^ n) := by
921	          rw [Units.val_pow_eq_pow_val, hu.unit_spec, ← hn, map_pow]
922	        have hyeq : y = algebraMap B Bg c * ↑(hu.unit ^ n)⁻¹ := by
923	          rw [ht_eq] at hct
924	          rw [← hct, mul_assoc, Units.mul_inv, mul_one]
925	        rw [hyeq]
926	        exact T.mul_mem (hrange c) (hpow_inv n)
927	      -- bump the base from `A_g` to `MvPoly A_g`.
928	      haveI : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g) (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (Localization.Away g))
929	          (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)) := by
930	        refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun r => ?_
931	        change algebraMap (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)) r
932	          = φ (MvPolynomial.C r)
933	        rw [hφ_def, MvPolynomial.aeval_C]
934	      have hle : Algebra.adjoin (Localization.Away g)
935	            ((algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))) '' (σ : Set B))
936	          ≤ (Algebra.adjoin (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (Localization.Away g))
937	              ((algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))) ''
938	                (σ : Set B))).restrictScalars
939	            (Localization.Away g) :=
940	        Algebra.adjoin_le Algebra.subset_adjoin
941	      rw [eq_top_iff]
942	      intro y _
943	      have hy : y ∈ (Algebra.adjoin (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (Localization.Away g))
944	          ((algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))) '' (σ : Set B))).restrictScalars
945	          (Localization.Away g) := by
946	        apply hle; rw [hAg]; trivial
947	      exact hy
948	    -- (III) assemble.
949	    have hfa : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (Localization.Away g))
950	        ↥(Algebra.adjoin (MvPolynomial (Fin s) (Localization.Away g))
951	          ((algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g))) '' (σ : Set B))) :=
952	      Algebra.finite_adjoin_of_finite_of_isIntegral
953	        (Set.Finite.image _ σ.finite_toSet)
954	        (by rintro _ ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩; exact hgenint x hx)
955	    rw [hadj] at hfa
956	    exact hfa.equiv (Subalgebra.topEquiv.toLinearEquiv)
957	  refine ⟨s, g, hgne, algAgBg, φ, hφ_inj, hfin, ?_⟩
958	  -- Compatibility of `algAgBg = (Localization.awayMap (algebraMap A B) g).toAlgebra`
959	  -- with the `A → B → B_g` tower, by the localisation-map commuting square.
960	  intro a
961	  change Localization.awayMap (algebraMap A B) g (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) a)
962	      = algebraMap B (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)) (algebraMap A B a)
963	  rw [Localization.awayMap, IsLocalization.Away.map, IsLocalization.map_eq]
964	
965	/-- **L5a — the generic-rank short exact sequence** (`lem:gf_generic_rank_ses`,
966	Nitsure §4 inductive step). For `A` a noetherian domain and `d ≥ 0`, a finite
967	module `N` over the polynomial ring `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `m ∈ ℕ`
968	and an injective `P_d`-linear map `φ : P_d^{⊕m} → N` whose cokernel
969	`T := N ⧸ range φ` is a torsion `P_d`-module. Equivalently, a short exact
970	sequence `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N → T → 0` with `T` torsion.
971	
972	`m` is the generic rank: `dim_{Frac P_d} (Frac P_d ⊗_{P_d} N)`. The map `φ`
973	is the `P_d`-linear combination of `m` lifts `v : Fin m → N` whose images form a
974	`Frac P_d`-basis of the localisation `N_Q := LocalizedModule (P_d)⁰ N`. This step
975	is built over `P_d` directly — no inversion of any `g ∈ A` is required. -/
976	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
977	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
978	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
979	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
980	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
981	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
982	        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
983	      Function.Injective φ ∧
984	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) := by
985	  classical
986	  -- Notation: `P = A[X_1,…,X_d]`, its non-zero divisors `S`, fraction field `K`,
987	  -- localisation `NK = N_Q`, and the localisation map `ℓ : N → NK`.
988	  let P := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A
989	  let S := nonZeroDivisors P
990	  let K := Localization S
991	  let NK := LocalizedModule S N
992	  let ℓ : N →ₗ[P] NK := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N
993	  -- `NK` is a finite-dimensional `K`-vector space; take a basis indexed by `Fin m`.
994	  let m := Module.finrank K NK
995	  let b : Module.Basis (Fin m) K NK := Module.finBasis K NK
996	  -- Lift each basis vector along `ℓ`, clearing its denominator.
997	  obtain ⟨lift, hlift⟩ :
998	      ∃ lift : Fin m → N × S, ∀ i, ((lift i).2 : P) • b i = ℓ (lift i).1 := by
999	    refine ⟨fun i => (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose, fun i => ?_⟩
1000	    exact (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose_spec
1001	  let v : Fin m → N := fun i => (lift i).1
1002	  -- The denominators map to units of `K`.
1003	  let u : Fin m → Kˣ := fun i => (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit
1004	  have hlv : ∀ i, ℓ (v i) = (↑(u i) : K) • b i := by
1005	    intro i
1006	    have hu : (↑(u i) : K) = algebraMap P K ((lift i).2 : P) :=
1007	      (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit_spec
1008	    rw [hu, algebraMap_smul]
1009	    exact (hlift i).symm
1010	  -- The images `ℓ (v i)` are linearly independent over `K` (unit multiples of a basis).
1011	  have hLIK : LinearIndependent K (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
1012	    have h := b.linearIndependent.units_smul u
1013	    have heq : (fun i => ℓ (v i)) = (u • (⇑b)) := by
1014	      funext i; rw [hlv i]; rfl
1015	    rw [heq]; exact h
1016	  -- Restrict scalars to `P` (the inclusion `P → K` is injective), then descend to `v`.
1017	  have hLIP : LinearIndependent P (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
1018	    refine hLIK.restrict_scalars ?_
1019	    intro x y hxy
1020	    apply IsFractionRing.injective P K
1021	    rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
1022	    exact hxy
1023	  have hLIv : LinearIndependent P v := LinearIndependent.of_comp ℓ hLIP
1024	  -- The map `φ : P^{⊕m} → N`, `e_i ↦ v i`.
1025	  refine ⟨m, Fintype.linearCombination P v, ?_, ?_⟩
1026	  · -- Injectivity of `φ` is exactly linear independence of `v`.
1027	    rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot']
1028	    intro g hg
1029	    rw [Fintype.linearCombination_apply] at hg
1030	    funext i
1031	    exact Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hLIv g hg i
1032	  · -- The cokernel `T = N ⧸ range φ` is torsion: every element is killed by a
1033	    -- non-zero divisor of `P`.
1034	    intro x
1035	    obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Submodule.Quotient.mk_surjective _ x
1036	    -- The images `ℓ (v i)` span `NK` over `K` (they are unit multiples of a basis).
1037	    have hspan : Submodule.span K (Set.range (fun i => ℓ (v i))) = ⊤ := by
1038	      refine top_unique ?_
1039	      rw [← b.span_eq, Submodule.span_le]
1040	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
1041	      have hbi : b i = (↑(u i) : K)⁻¹ • ℓ (v i) := by
1042	        rw [hlv i, smul_smul, inv_mul_cancel₀ (u i).ne_zero, one_smul]
1043	      rw [hbi]
1044	      exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
1045	    -- Hence `ℓ n` is a `K`-linear combination of the `ℓ (v i)`.
1046	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := (Submodule.mem_span_range_iff_exists_fun K).mp
1047	      (show ℓ n ∈ Submodule.span K (Set.range fun i => ℓ (v i)) by
1048	        rw [hspan]; exact Submodule.mem_top)
1049	    -- Clear the denominators of the finitely many coefficients `c i`.
1050	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples S Finset.univ c
1051	    have ha : ∀ i, ∃ y : P, algebraMap P K y = (s : P) • c i := fun i =>
1052	      hs i (Finset.mem_univ i)
1053	    choose a hae using ha
1054	    -- After multiplying by `s`, `ℓ (s • n)` becomes `ℓ (φ a)`.
1055	    have key : ℓ ((s : P) • n) = ℓ (Fintype.linearCombination P v a) := by
1056	      rw [map_smul, ← hc, Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]
1057	      refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1058	      rw [map_smul]
1059	      -- `(s:P) • (c i • ℓ v_i) = a i • ℓ v_i`
1060	      rw [← algebraMap_smul K ((s : P)) (c i • ℓ (v i)), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def,
1061	        ← hae i, algebraMap_smul]
1062	    -- So `s • n - φ a` lies in the kernel of `ℓ`, hence is killed by some `t ∈ S`.
1063	    have hker : ℓ ((s : P) • n - Fintype.linearCombination P v a) = 0 := by
1064	      rw [map_sub, key, sub_self]
1065	    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S ℓ).mp hker
1066	    -- The non-zero divisor `t * s` annihilates the class of `n` in `T`.
1067	    refine ⟨t * s, ?_⟩
1068	    rw [smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at ht
1069	    have ht2 : (↑t : P) • ((↑s : P) • n) = (↑t : P) • Fintype.linearCombination P v a := ht
1070	    have hmem : (↑(t * s) : P) • n ∈ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination P v) := by
1071	      rw [Submonoid.coe_mul, mul_smul, ht2, ← map_smul]
1072	      exact LinearMap.mem_range_self _ _
1073	    -- Conclude `(t * s) • ⟦n⟧ = 0`.
1074	    rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero]
1075	    exact hmem
1076	
1077	/-- **L5b.1 — annihilator extraction for the torsion module**
1078	(`lem:gf_torsion_annihilator`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 0`,
1079	and `T` a finite *torsion* module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exists a
1080	non-zero `F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`. Since `P_d` is a domain, a non-zero-divisor
1081	annihilator (provided by `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`) is the
1082	same thing as a non-zero one. -/
1083	theorem gf_torsion_annihilator
1084	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (d : ℕ)
1085	    (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1086	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1087	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
1088	    ∃ F : MvPolynomial (Fin d) A, F ≠ 0 ∧
1089	      F ∈ Module.annihilator (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T := by
1090	  obtain ⟨F, hFann, hFnzd⟩ := Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors htors
1091	  refine ⟨F, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hFnzd, ?_⟩
1092	  rw [← Submodule.annihilator_top]
1093	  exact hFann
1094	
1095	/-! ### L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables (`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`)
1096	
1097	The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
1098	change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
1099	unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
1100	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation
1101	`T` and proves the leading coefficient is a unit — but only over a *field*, and as
1102	`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
1103	(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a
1104	domain it is merely non-zero, and becomes a unit after inverting it). The degree
1105	bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
1106	a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/
1107	
1108	section NagataNormalization
1109	
1110	-- `AlgebraicGeometry.Polynomial` exists and would shadow the root namespace, so the
1111	-- `Polynomial`/`MvPolynomial` lemma APIs are opened with explicit `_root_.` prefixes.
1112	open _root_.Polynomial _root_.MvPolynomial _root_.Ideal Nat _root_.RingHom List
1113	
1114	/-- `finSuccEquiv` commutes with coefficient base change `MvPolynomial.map phi`. -/
1115	private theorem finSuccEquiv_map_comm {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
1116	    (phi : A →+* B) (m : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :
1117	    finSuccEquiv B m (MvPolynomial.map phi q)
1118	      = Polynomial.map (MvPolynomial.map phi) (finSuccEquiv A m q) := by
1119	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1120	  | C a => simp [finSuccEquiv_apply]
1121	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1122	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1123	    simp only [map_mul, Polynomial.map_mul, hp]
1124	    congr 1
1125	    cases i using Fin.cases with
1126	    | zero => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_zero]
1127	    | succ j => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ]
1128	
1129	variable {k : Type*} [CommRing k] [IsDomain k] {n : ℕ} (f : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k)
1130	variable (v w : Fin (n + 1) →₀ ℕ)
1131	
1132	local notation3 "up" => 2 + f.totalDegree
1133	local notation3 "r" => fun (i : Fin (n + 1)) ↦ up ^ i.1
1134	
1135	omit [IsDomain k] in
1136	variable {f v} in
1137	private lemma lt_up (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) : ∀ l ∈ ofFn v, l < up := by grind
1138	
1139	/-- The triangular Nagata substitution `X_i ↦ X_i + c • X_0 ^ (up ^ i)` (`i ≠ 0`),
1140	`X_0 ↦ X_0`. Transcribed from `Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization` to a domain. -/
1141	private noncomputable abbrev T1 (c : k) :
1142	    MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k →ₐ[k] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k :=
1143	  aeval fun i ↦ if i = 0 then X 0 else X i + c • X 0 ^ r i
1144	
1145	omit [IsDomain k] in
1146	private lemma t1_comp_t1_neg (c : k) : (T1 f c).comp (T1 f (-c)) = AlgHom.id _ _ := by
1147	  rw [comp_aeval, ← MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]; ext i v; cases i using Fin.cases <;> simp
1148	
1149	/-- The Nagata transformation as an `A`-algebra automorphism. -/
1150	private noncomputable abbrev T := AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (T1 f 1) (T1 f (-1))
1151	  (t1_comp_t1_neg f 1) (by simpa using t1_comp_t1_neg f (-1))
1152	
1153	omit [IsDomain k] in
1154	private lemma sum_r_mul_ne (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) (wlt : ∀ i, w i < up) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1155	    ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * v x ≠ ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * w x := by
1156	  intro h
1157	  refine ne <| Finsupp.ext <| congrFun <| ofFn_inj.mp ?_
1158	  apply ofDigits_inj_of_len_eq (Nat.lt_add_right f.totalDegree one_lt_two)
1159	    (by simp) (lt_up vlt) (lt_up wlt)
1160	  simpa only [ofDigits_eq_sum_mapIdx, mapIdx_eq_ofFn, get_ofFn, length_ofFn,
1161	    Fin.val_cast, mul_comm, sum_ofFn] using h
1162	
1163	private lemma degreeOf_zero_t {a : k} (ha : a ≠ 0) : ((T f) (monomial v a)).degreeOf 0 =
1164	    ∑ i : Fin (n + 1), (r i) * v i := by
1165	  rw [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv, monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_pow v fun a ↦ X a]
1166	  simp only [Fin.prod_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_succ, map_mul, map_prod, map_pow,
1167	    AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, MvPolynomial.aeval_C, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, if_pos, Fin.succ_ne_zero,
1168	    ite_false, one_smul, map_add, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, finSuccEquiv_X_succ, algebraMap_eq]
1169	  have h (i : Fin n) :
1170	      (Polynomial.C (X (R := k) i) + Polynomial.X ^ r i.succ) ^ v i.succ ≠ 0 :=
1171	    pow_ne_zero (v i.succ) (leadingCoeff_ne_zero.mp <| by simp [add_comm, leadingCoeff_X_pow_add_C])
1172	  rw [natDegree_mul (by simp [ha]) (mul_ne_zero (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr
1173	    (fun i _ ↦ h i))), natDegree_mul (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun i _ ↦ h i)),
1174	    natDegree_prod _ _ (fun i _ ↦ h i), natDegree_finSuccEquiv, degreeOf_C]
1175	  simpa only [natDegree_pow, zero_add, natDegree_X, mul_one, Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_mul,
1176	    add_right_inj] using Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ ↦ by
1177	    rw [add_comm (Polynomial.C _), natDegree_X_pow_add_C, mul_comm])
1178	
1179	private lemma degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne (hv : v ∈ f.support) (hw : w ∈ f.support) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1180	    (T f <| monomial v <| coeff v f).degreeOf 0 ≠
1181	    (T f <| monomial w <| coeff w f).degreeOf 0 := by
1182	  rw [degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hv, degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hw]
1183	  refine sum_r_mul_ne f v w (fun i ↦ ?_) (fun i ↦ ?_) ne <;>
1184	  exact lt_of_le_of_lt ((monomial_le_degreeOf i ‹_›).trans (degreeOf_le_totalDegree f i)) (by lia)
1185	
1186	private lemma leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t :
1187	    (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) ((monomial v) (coeff v f)))).leadingCoeff =
1188	    algebraMap k _ (coeff v f) := by
1189	  rw [monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_fintype]
1190	  · simp only [map_mul, map_prod, leadingCoeff_mul, leadingCoeff_prod]
1191	    rw [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, algHom_C, algebraMap_eq, finSuccEquiv_apply, eval₂Hom_C, coe_comp]
1192	    simp only [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, Function.comp_apply, leadingCoeff_C, map_pow,
1193	      leadingCoeff_pow, algebraMap_eq]
1194	    have : ∀ j, ((finSuccEquiv k n) ((T1 f) 1 (X j))).leadingCoeff = 1 := fun j ↦ by
1195	      by_cases h : j = 0
1196	      · simp [h, finSuccEquiv_apply]
1197	      · simp only [aeval_eq_bind₁, bind₁_X_right, if_neg h, one_smul, map_add, map_pow]
1198	        obtain ⟨i, rfl⟩ := Fin.exists_succ_eq.mpr h
1199	        simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, add_comm]
1200	    simp only [this, one_pow, Finset.prod_const_one, mul_one]
1201	  exact fun i ↦ pow_zero _
1202	
1203	/-- Over a domain, the `T`-transform of a non-zero `f` has, in `X_0`, a leading
1204	coefficient equal to `C (coeff v f)` for some `v ∈ f.support` (hence non-zero). The
1205	Mathlib original concludes `IsUnit` using the field hypothesis; here we keep the exact
1206	coefficient so the caller can invert it. -/
1207	private lemma T_leadingcoeff_eq (fne : f ≠ 0) :
1208	    ∃ v ∈ f.support,
1209	      (finSuccEquiv k n (T f f)).leadingCoeff = MvPolynomial.C (coeff v f) := by
1210	  obtain ⟨v, vin, vs⟩ := Finset.exists_max_image f.support
1211	    (fun v ↦ (T f ((monomial v) (coeff v f))).degreeOf 0) (support_nonempty.mpr fne)
1212	  set h := fun w ↦ (MvPolynomial.monomial w) (coeff w f)
1213	  simp only [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv] at vs
1214	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <
1215	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).degree := by
1216	    intro x hx
1217	    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
1218	    apply degree_lt_degree <| lt_of_le_of_ne (vs x h1) ?_
1219	    simpa only [natDegree_finSuccEquiv]
1220	      using degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne f _ _ h1 vin <| ne_of_not_mem_cons h2
1221	  have coeff : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v + ∑ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, h x))).leadingCoeff =
1222	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).leadingCoeff := by
1223	    simp only [map_add, map_sum]
1224	    rw [add_comm]
1225	    apply leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| (lt_of_le_of_lt <| degree_sum_le _ _) ?_
1226	    have h2 : h v ≠ 0 := by simpa [h] using mem_support_iff.mp vin
1227	    replace h2 : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))) ≠ 0 := fun eq ↦ h2 <|
1228	      by simpa only [map_eq_zero_iff _ (AlgEquiv.injective _)] using eq

1243	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (m : ℕ)
1244	    (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
1245	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
1246	      (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A),
1247	      IsUnit

1298	`S`-algebra isomorphism induced by `finSuccEquiv`. -/
1299	theorem mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar
1300	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
1301	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
1302	    (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
1303	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1304	        (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
1305	          MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite := by
1306	  letI algI : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) :=
1307	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1308	      (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom).toAlgebra
1309	  have hc : IsUnit ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
1310	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit
1311	  have hcP :
1312	      IsUnit (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)) :=
1313	    hc.map Polynomial.C
1314	  have hmonic : (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1315	      * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).Monic := by
1316	    rw [Polynomial.Monic, Polynomial.leadingCoeff_C_mul_of_isUnit hc]
1317	    exact Units.inv_mul_of_eq hp.unit_spec
1318	  have hspan : Ideal.span {Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) :
1319	        MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1320	      = Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p} :=
1321	    Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit hcP (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p)
1322	  have hfinstd : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1323	      (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) := by
1324	    rw [← hspan]; exact hmonic.finite_quotient

1327	          MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R →+* Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R))
1328	        (Ideal.span {p}) := by
1329	    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl

1331	      ≃+* (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=

1335	      ψ (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1336	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) s)
1337	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1338	          (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p})

1341	    change ψ (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s))

1348	    AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv hsmul

1354	/-- Pull back an `R`-module structure along an additive equivalence `e : M ≃+ N`, defining
1355	`r • y := e (r • e.symm y)`. With this structure `e` is `R`-linear; used in the torsion
1356	reindex to transport the reindexed module structure from the `P`-localisation of the torsion
1357	module to the goal's `A`-localisation. -/
1358	@[reducible] def pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1359	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] (e : M ≃+ N) : Module R N where
1360	  smul r y := e (r • e.symm y)
1361	  one_smul y := by change e (1 • e.symm y) = y; rw [one_smul, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1362	  mul_smul r s y := by
1363	    change e ((r * s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm (e (s • e.symm y)))
1364	    rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, mul_smul]
1365	  smul_zero r := by change e (r • e.symm 0) = 0; rw [map_zero, smul_zero, map_zero]
1366	  smul_add r x y := by
1367	    change e (r • e.symm (x + y)) = e (r • e.symm x) + e (r • e.symm y)
1368	    rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add]
1369	  add_smul r s y := by
1370	    change e ((r + s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm y) + e (s • e.symm y)
1371	    rw [add_smul, map_add]
1372	  zero_smul y := by change e (0 • e.symm y) = 0; rw [zero_smul, map_zero]
1373	
1374	/-- Module-finiteness transports across the pulled-back structure of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1375	if `M` is a finite `R`-module then so is `N` under the pulled-back action. -/
1376	theorem finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1377	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] (e : M ≃+ N) :
1378	    @Module.Finite R N _ _ (pullbackModuleAddEquiv e) := by
1379	  letI : Module R N := pullbackModuleAddEquiv e
1380	  refine Module.Finite.equiv (M := M) (N := N)
1381	    { toFun := e, map_add' := map_add e, invFun := e.symm,
1382	      left_inv := e.left_inv, right_inv := e.right_inv,
1383	      map_smul' := fun r x => ?_ }
1384	  simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1385	  change e (r • x) = e (r • e.symm (e x))
1386	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
1387	
1388	/-- A scalar tower transports across the pulled-back structures of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1389	if `e : M ≃+ N` and `M` carries compatible `Ag`- and `R`-actions (`IsScalarTower Ag R M`), then
1390	the pulled-back `Ag`- and `R`-actions on `N` form a scalar tower as well. -/
1391	theorem pullback_isScalarTower {Ag R M N : Type*} [CommSemiring Ag] [Semiring R] [Algebra Ag R]
1392	    [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module Ag M] [IsScalarTower Ag R M]
1393	    (e : M ≃+ N) :
1394	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1395	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1396	    IsScalarTower Ag R N := by
1397	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1398	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1399	  refine ⟨fun a b x => ?_⟩
1400	  change e ((a • b) • e.symm x) = e (a • e.symm (e (b • e.symm x)))
1401	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, smul_assoc]

1403	/-- Transport module-finiteness across a ring isomorphism of the acting ring that is
1404	compatible with given `R`-algebra structures. If `ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂` satisfies
1405	`ψ ∘ algebraMap R B₁ = algebraMap R B₂`, `B₂` is module-finite over `R`, and `M` is
1406	module-finite over `B₁` (with the scalar tower `R → B₁ → M`), then `M` is module-finite
1407	over `R`. -/
1408	theorem finite_of_quotientRingEquiv
1409	    {R B₁ B₂ M : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B₁] [CommRing B₂] [AddCommGroup M]
1410	    [Algebra R B₁] [Algebra R B₂] [Module B₁ M] [Module R M] [IsScalarTower R B₁ M]
1411	    (ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂)
1412	    (hψ : ∀ r : R, ψ (algebraMap R B₁ r) = algebraMap R B₂ r)
1413	    (hB₂ : Module.Finite R B₂) (hM : Module.Finite B₁ M) :
1414	    Module.Finite R M := by
1415	  haveI := hB₂
1416	  haveI := hM
1417	  have hsymm : ∀ r : R, ψ.symm (algebraMap R B₂ r) = algebraMap R B₁ r := by
1418	    intro r; rw [← hψ r, ψ.symm_apply_apply]
1419	  haveI : Module.Finite R B₁ :=
1420	    Module.Finite.equiv (AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := ψ.symm) hsymm).toLinearEquiv

1423	/-- Descent of a localized-module structure along a scalar tower. If `f : M →ₗ[R] M'`
1424	localises `M` at the image submonoid `S.map (algebraMap R' R)` over `R`, then its
1425	restriction of scalars to `R'` localises `M` at `S` over `R'`. -/
1426	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars
1427	    {R' R M M' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [CommRing R] [Algebra R' R]
1428	    [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R' M] [Module R M] [Module R' M'] [Module R M']
1429	    [IsScalarTower R' R M] [IsScalarTower R' R M']
1430	    (S : Submonoid R') (f : M →ₗ[R] M')
1431	    [IsLocalizedModule (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f] :
1432	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R') := by
1433	  apply IsLocalizedModule.mk
1434	  · intro s
1435	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1436	    have hmem : algebraMap R' R (s : R') ∈ S.map (algebraMap R' R) := ⟨s, s.2, rfl⟩
1437	    have hu := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f ⟨algebraMap R' R (s : R'), hmem⟩
1438	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hu
1439	    have hfun : ⇑((algebraMap R' (Module.End R' M')) (s : R'))
1440	        = ⇑((algebraMap R (Module.End R M')) (algebraMap R' R (s : R'))) := by
1441	      funext x
1442	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
1443	    rw [hfun]
1444	    exact hu
1445	  · intro y
1446	    obtain ⟨⟨t, sm⟩, hsm⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f y
1447	    obtain ⟨s, hsS, hs⟩ := sm.2
1448	    refine ⟨⟨t, ⟨s, hsS⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1449	    change (s : R') • y = (f.restrictScalars R') t
1450	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') y, hs]
1451	    exact hsm
1452	  · intro x₁ x₂ h
1453	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply, LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply] at h
1454	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S.map (algebraMap R' R)) (f := f) h

1456	    refine ⟨⟨s, hsS⟩, ?_⟩
1457	    change (s : R') • x₁ = (s : R') • x₂
1458	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₁,
1459	        ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₂, hs]

1473	theorem gf_torsion_reindex
1474	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1475	    (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
1476	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1477	    [Module A T] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]

1479	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
1480	      (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
1481	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
1482	      (_ : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
1483	              (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
1484	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
1485	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))

1492	  -- non-zero `g ∈ A` making `e F` monic in `X₀` up to a unit after inverting `g`.

1498	    (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F)) hunit

1506	  --           `A_g := Localization.Away g`, `MC := Submonoid.map C (powers g) ⊆ P`.

1508	      (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) :=
1509	    (MvPolynomial.algebraMvPolynomial :
1510	      Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)))
1511	  set MC : Submonoid (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :=

1513	  haveI hPgloc : IsLocalization MC (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) :=
1514	    MvPolynomial.isLocalization (Submonoid.powers g) (Localization.Away g)

1806	    change σ (σ.symm c) • x = c • x
1807	    rw [AlgEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1808	  have hDfree : Module.Free (Localization.Away (g * a))
1809	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)) :=
1810	    Module.Free.of_basis
1811	      ((Module.Free.chooseBasis (Localization.Away h)
1812	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h)
1813	          (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))).mapCoeffs σ.symm.toRingEquiv hcompat)
1814	  -- The `A`-linear `ε` upgrades to `A_{g·a}`-linear; conclude freeness of `T_{g·a}`.
1815	  have εL : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (g * a)) T ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (g * a)]
1816	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) :=
1817	    LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (g * a))
1818	      (Localization.Away (g * a)) ε
1819	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv' hDfree εL.symm
